prerequisites: A 7shifts account with API access enabled.
An active company administrator who has logged in at least once, to serve as the access token's technical contact.
An access token generated under Company Settings > Developer Tools (provides the required access_token field).
A start date to define the earliest point from which to sync data (the required start_date field).

authMethods.name: Access Token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: 7shifts personal Access Tokens are the authentication method for internal/non-partner integrations and map to this connector's required access_token secret field. Tokens grant administrator-level access and are sent in the Authorization header using the bearer scheme (Authorization: Bearer {ACCESS_TOKEN}). Note that authentication is administrator-level and cannot be scoped to specific resources or actions.

setupSteps.title: Generate an access token in 7shifts
setupSteps.body: Sign in to 7shifts as a company admin. Navigate to Company Settings, then Developer Tools. Under the Access Token section, click Create Access Token, give it a descriptive name, and assign a technical contact who is an active company admin and has logged in at least once. The token is assigned the latest API version by default. Copy the token and store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Provide the access token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated token in the access_token field. The connector sends it as a bearer token in the Authorization header on every request. If the token's technical-contact admin is later deactivated or loses admin status, the token stops authenticating until a valid admin is reassigned.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should pull records. Data dated before this point is not synced.

sources.title: How To Generate an Access Token - 7shifts Help Center
